Apple Scab Leaf.symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves, distortion and early leaf drop
Apple Scab Leaf.favorable_conditions: Cool wet weather, 15–24°C with prolonged leaf wetness

chemical.applications: 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals starting at bud burst
Begin at pink bud stage, repeat every 7–10 days

organic.notes: Prepare fresh; apply at bud swell and after petal fall
Add 1 ml Teepol as emulsifier; spray in early morning or evening

Apple Scab Leaf.ipm_notes: Rake and destroy fallen leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation. Plant resistant varieties like Florina.
Apple Scab Leaf.source: ICAR-CISH, NRC on Apple Solan recommendations

Apple leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Apple leaf.ipm_notes: Continue regular scouting every 7 days. Maintain balanced NPK nutrition. Monitor for early signs of scab or rust.

Apple rust leaf.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface, tube-like structures on underside
Apple rust leaf.favorable_conditions: Wet spring conditions, proximity to juniper/cedar alternate hosts

chemical.applications: 2–3 sprays starting at pink bud stage
Apply at pink bud; repeat at 10-day intervals

organic.notes: Do not apply when temperature exceeds 32°C; phytotoxicity risk

Apple rust leaf.ipm_notes: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees which are alternate hosts. Prune and destroy infected shoots.
Apple rust leaf.source: ICAR-CISH Lucknow, Apple Pest and Disease Management Bulletin

Bell_pepper leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Bell_pepper leaf.ipm_notes: Monitor weekly for thrips (vector of TSWV), aphids, and early blight symptoms. Maintain soil moisture consistency to prevent blossom end rot.

Bell_pepper leaf spot.symptoms: Water-soaked spots turning brown with yellow halo, defoliation under heavy infection
Bell_pepper leaf spot.favorable_conditions: Warm humid weather 25–30°C, rain splash, wounds from insects

chemical.applications: Spray at first symptom appearance; repeat every 10 days
2–3 sprays at 10-day intervals; do not use more than 3 times per season

organic.notes: Apply preventively during humid periods
Soil drenching at transplanting stage for root protection

Bell_pepper leaf spot.ipm_notes: Use certified disease-free seed. Avoid overhead irrigation. Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2 years.
Bell_pepper leaf spot.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi, Pepper Disease Management; TNAU Agritech Portal

Blueberry leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Blueberry leaf.ipm_notes: Blueberry is not a major crop in AP/Telangana. Maintain soil pH 4.5–5.5 for optimal growth. Scout for mummy berry and botrytis in humid conditions.

Cherry leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Cherry leaf.ipm_notes: Monitor for cherry leaf spot (Blumeriella jaapii) during wet seasons. Avoid wetting foliage during irrigation.

Corn Gray leaf spot.symptoms: Long rectangular gray-tan lesions parallel to leaf veins, premature death of leaves
Corn Gray leaf spot.favorable_conditions: High humidity >80%, temperature 25–30°C, minimum tillage fields with crop residue

chemical.applications: Apply at VT (tasseling) stage; repeat after 14 days if needed
One spray at tasseling; highly effective as preventive

organic.notes: Foliar application at vegetative stage; repeat at 15-day intervals

Corn Gray leaf spot.ipm_notes: Deep ploughing to bury infected residue. Use resistant hybrids (DHM-117, HQPM-1). Avoid late planting.
Corn Gray leaf spot.source: ICAR-IIMR Ludhiana, Maize Disease Management Bulletin 2022

Corn leaf blight.symptoms: Large cigar-shaped grayish-green lesions 5–15 cm long on leaves, starting from lower leaves
Corn leaf blight.favorable_conditions: Moderate temperature 18–27°C, heavy dew, high humidity, dense canopy

chemical.applications: First spray at disease onset; 2–3 sprays at 10-day intervals
At tasseling stage; 1–2 sprays

organic.notes: Soil application at sowing; seed treatment at 4 g per kg seed

Corn leaf blight.ipm_notes: Use resistant varieties (HQPM-5, Vivek Hybrid 9). Destroy infected crop debris. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Corn leaf blight.source: ICAR-IIMR Ludhiana, DRR Technical Bulletin on Maize Diseases

Corn rust leaf.symptoms: Small, round to elongated brick-red pustules on both leaf surfaces, powdery spore masses
Corn rust leaf.favorable_conditions: Cool temperature 16–23°C, high humidity, dew on leaves

chemical.applications: 2–3 sprays at 10-day intervals at first pustule appearance
Single application at early rust detection

organic.notes: Apply in early morning; avoid application above 32°C

Corn rust leaf.ipm_notes: Plant early to escape late-season rust build-up. Use resistant hybrids. Avoid excess nitrogen application.
Corn rust leaf.source: ICAR-IIMR Ludhiana; TNAU Agritech Portal — Maize Rust Management

Peach leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Peach leaf.ipm_notes: Watch for peach leaf curl (Taphrina deformans) in early spring. Apply copper fungicide at leaf fall and before bud swell as preventive measure.

Potato leaf early blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots (target-board pattern) on older leaves, yellow halo around lesions
Potato leaf early blight.favorable_conditions: Warm days 24–29°C, cool nights, humidity >90%, plants under stress

chemical.applications: Spray at first symptom; repeat every 7–10 days, 4–5 sprays total
Preventive spray during humid periods; 3–4 sprays

organic.notes: Certified for organic use; apply before disease onset in humid conditions
Reduces spore germination; apply every 10 days

Potato leaf early blight.ipm_notes: Ensure balanced potassium nutrition to increase resistance. Avoid overhead irrigation. Destroy volunteer potato plants.
Potato leaf early blight.source: ICAR-CPRI Shimla, Potato Disease Management Bulletin 2021

Potato leaf late blight.symptoms: Water-soaked pale green lesions rapidly turning dark brown, white cottony growth on leaf underside in humid conditions
Potato leaf late blight.favorable_conditions: Cool temperature 10–21°C, high humidity >90%, prolonged leaf wetness — spreads explosively

chemical.applications: At first blight appearance; repeat every 7 days — critical to not delay
Preventive spray at canopy closure; 3–4 sprays at 7-day intervals

organic.notes: Apply preventively; highly effective when used before disease outbreak

Potato leaf late blight.ipm_notes: Use certified disease-free seed tubers. Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Bahar). Avoid irrigation in evening. Destroy infected haulms before harvest.
Potato leaf late blight.source: ICAR-CPRI Shimla, Late Blight Management Guidelines; CACP Potato Report 2023

Raspberry leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Raspberry leaf.ipm_notes: Raspberry is not a common AP/Telangana crop. If grown, watch for Raspberry leaf spot (Sphaerulina rubi) and cane blight during wet seasons.

Soyabean leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Soyabean leaf.ipm_notes: Scout weekly for sudden death syndrome, frogeye leaf spot, and soybean rust. Monitor for whitefly and thrips vectors during pod filling.

Squash Powdery mildew leaf.symptoms: White powdery coating on upper and lower leaf surfaces, leaf yellowing and premature senescence
Squash Powdery mildew leaf.favorable_conditions: Warm dry days 20–28°C, high humidity at night, poor air circulation

chemical.applications: At first symptom; repeat every 10 days, 3–4 sprays
2–3 sprays at 10-day intervals

organic.notes: Add Teepol as emulsifier; spray in early morning
5 g baking soda per litre of water; alters leaf surface pH to inhibit fungus

Squash Powdery mildew leaf.ipm_notes: Ensure wide row spacing for air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen. Remove heavily infected leaves.
Squash Powdery mildew leaf.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi, Cucurbit Disease Management; TNAU Agritech Portal

Strawberry leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Strawberry leaf.ipm_notes: Monitor for common leaf spot (Mycosphaerella fragariae) and angular leaf spot (Xanthomonas fragariae). Avoid water on foliage.

Tomato Early blight leaf.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots with yellow halo on older leaves, moves upward
Tomato Early blight leaf.favorable_conditions: Temperature 24–29°C, humidity >90%, plants under stress or nutrient deficiency

chemical.applications: At first symptom; 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals
2 sprays at 14-day intervals; rotate with mancozeb to prevent resistance

organic.notes: 3 g per litre; apply at bud initiation stage preventively
Soil drenching at transplanting; reduces soil-borne Alternaria inoculum

Tomato Early blight leaf.ipm_notes: Ensure adequate potassium (K2O) fertilization. Remove and destroy lower infected leaves. Stake plants for better aeration.
Tomato Early blight leaf.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi, Tomato Production Technology; TNAU Agritech Portal

Tomato Septoria leaf spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark border and light gray center, dark spore-producing bodies in center of spots
Tomato Septoria leaf spot.favorable_conditions: Warm humid conditions 20–25°C, prolonged wetness, rain splash spreads spores

chemical.applications: At first symptom appearance; repeat every 7–10 days
Alternate with Chlorothalonil; 3–4 sprays total

organic.notes: Apply preventively; effective when started before disease establishment

Tomato Septoria leaf spot.ipm_notes: Remove lower leaves showing first symptoms. Mulch soil to reduce rain splash. Avoid working in wet foliage. Use drip irrigation.
Tomato Septoria leaf spot.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi; TNAU Agritech Portal — Tomato Disease Management

Tomato leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
Tomato leaf.ipm_notes: Maintain weekly scouting. Watch for early blight symptoms on lower leaves, curling (virus indicator), and white fly colonies on leaf undersides. Ensure consistent drip irrigation.

Tomato leaf bacterial spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots turning dark brown with greasy appearance, raised lesions on fruit surface
Tomato leaf bacterial spot.favorable_conditions: Warm wet weather 24–30°C, rain, overhead irrigation, infected seed

chemical.applications: At first symptom; 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals

chemical.dilution: 100 mg (0.1 g) per litre of water
chemical.applications: 2 sprays at 10-day interval; avoid overuse — antibiotic resistance risk

organic.notes: Apply preventively during wet seasons; best results before infection establishes
Foliar spray; biocontrol agent producing antibacterial compounds

Tomato leaf bacterial spot.ipm_notes: Use certified disease-free seed. Hot water seed treatment: 52°C for 30 minutes. Switch to drip irrigation. Rotate with non-solanaceous crops.
Tomato leaf bacterial spot.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi, Tomato Bacterial Diseases; CIB&RC registration data

Tomato leaf late blight.symptoms: Greasy water-soaked lesions rapidly turning dark brown, white mold on leaf undersides, characteristic foul smell
Tomato leaf late blight.favorable_conditions: Cool nights <12°C, warm days, humidity >90%, rain — spreads extremely rapidly

chemical.applications: At FIRST appearance — do not delay; repeat every 5–7 days
2 applications max per season; highly systemic — use in rotation

organic.notes: Apply preventively; once late blight establishes, organic options are limited

Tomato leaf late blight.ipm_notes: Remove and burn infected plant parts immediately. Avoid working in wet fields. Spray in morning so leaves dry by evening. This is the most destructive tomato disease — act fast.
Tomato leaf late blight.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi; CPRI Shimla Late Blight Monitoring Network guidelines

Tomato leaf mosaic virus.symptoms: Light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves, leaf distortion, stunting, reduced fruit set
Tomato leaf mosaic virus.favorable_conditions: Spreads by contact, contaminated tools, infected seed — not insect-vectored

chemical.active_ingredient: No curative chemical available for virus
chemical.applications: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately

organic.notes: Controls sucking pests that stress plants; does not cure virus

Tomato leaf mosaic virus.ipm_notes: No chemical cure for viral diseases. Rogue out infected plants immediately. Disinfect hands and tools with soap or 1% sodium hypochlorite. Use virus-indexed seed. Control aphids and thrips as secondary vectors.
Tomato leaf mosaic virus.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi, Viral Disease Management in Tomato 2022

Tomato leaf yellow virus.pathogen: Begomovirus transmitted by Bemisia tabaci whitefly (virus)
Tomato leaf yellow virus.symptoms: Upward leaf curling, yellowing of leaf margins, stunted growth, complete crop loss in severe cases
Tomato leaf yellow virus.favorable_conditions: High whitefly population, temperature 25–35°C, dry conditions favour whitefly vector

chemical.application_method: Foliar spray or soil drench at transplanting
chemical.applications: Seed treatment + 2 foliar sprays at 15-day intervals; do not use more than 2 foliar applications
Rotate with imidacloprid; 2–3 sprays at 15-day intervals

organic.notes: Install at crop canopy height for whitefly monitoring and mass trapping

organic.dosage_per_acre: 1 litre neem oil in 200 litres water
organic.notes: Repels whitefly; apply every 7 days

Tomato leaf yellow virus.ipm_notes: TYLCV has NO cure — control is entirely through vector (whitefly) management. Rogue out infected plants. Use reflective silver mulch to repel whiteflies. Plant virus-resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak, Pusa Rohini). This is the most damaging tomato virus in AP/Telangana.
Tomato leaf yellow virus.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi; ANGRAU, Hyderabad — TYLCV Management Advisory

Tomato mold leaf.symptoms: Pale green to yellow spots on upper leaf surface, olive-brown velvety mold on underside
Tomato mold leaf.favorable_conditions: High humidity >85%, temperature 22–25°C, poor ventilation — common in greenhouse/polyhouse tomato

chemical.applications: 3–4 sprays at 7-day intervals
2 sprays at 14-day intervals

organic.notes: Competitive exclusion; spray on leaf undersides where mold develops

Tomato mold leaf.ipm_notes: Improve ventilation in polyhouse. Maintain humidity below 85%. Remove lower infected leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation. Stake plants to improve air flow.
Tomato mold leaf.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi; NHB Protected Cultivation Guidelines 2021

Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.pathogen: Tetranychus urticae (arachnid pest — not a fungal disease)
Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.symptoms: Fine stippling/bronzing on upper leaf surface, fine webbing on leaf undersides, leaf desiccation in severe cases
Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.favorable_conditions: Hot dry conditions >30°C, low humidity, dusty conditions — population explosions in summer

chemical.application_method: Foliar spray — thorough coverage including undersides
chemical.applications: 2 sprays at 7-day intervals; highly effective but rotate to prevent resistance
1–2 applications; ovicidal action kills eggs and larvae

organic.notes: Disrupts mite moulting; apply every 5–7 days for 3 cycles
3 g per litre; contact miticide — do not apply above 35°C

Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.ipm_notes: Note: This is a pest (mite), not a fungal disease — fungicides are ineffective. Release predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis) as biocontrol. Avoid dusty field conditions. Do not overuse broad-spectrum insecticides which kill natural enemies.
Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.source: ICAR-IIVR Varanasi; NHB IPM Guidelines for Tomato 2022; CIB&RC registered miticides list

grape leaf.symptoms: No disease detected. Leaf appears healthy.
grape leaf.ipm_notes: Scout for downy mildew (Plasmopara viticola) and powdery mildew (Uncinula necator) which are the major threats to grapes in AP/Telangana. Apply protective copper sprays before monsoon onset.

grape leaf black rot.symptoms: Circular reddish-brown leaf lesions with dark border, black shriveled mummified berries
grape leaf black rot.favorable_conditions: Warm wet weather 22–29°C during flowering and fruit set, rain splash, infected mummified berries from previous season

chemical.applications: Begin at bud break; 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals through berry set
2 sprays: at 50% bloom and 10 days after

organic.notes: Traditional and highly effective; apply from bud break through berry set

grape leaf black rot.ipm_notes: Remove and destroy all mummified berries and infected canes during dormant pruning — primary inoculum source. Ensure good canopy management for air circulation. This disease can cause 100% crop loss in unsprayed vineyards.
grape leaf black rot.source: NRC for Grapes Pune (ICAR-NRC Grapes), Grape Protection Bulletin; TNAU Agritech Portal
